steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a completion that synthesizes both studies. Hart found investment increased in the three years after liberalization; Collins and Menon found those gains did not endure over the full 1976–1993 period. The phrase "Taken together, these results suggest" requires a conclusion that captures both findings.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — investment growth is likely to be more consistent in countries that liberalize than in countries that do not.: contradicts Collins and Menon, who found effects did not endure. ✗
  • Choice B — economists' expectations about the effect of liberalization on investment were largely correct.: the passage makes no mention of what economists predicted. ✗
  • Choice C — companies typically do not benefit from liberalization until at least three years after liberalization occurs.: reverses the timeline — Hart shows benefits come immediately, not after a delay. ✗
  • Choice D — liberalization may provide a boost to investment that fades over time.: directly reconciles both studies — a short-term rise (Hart) that diminishes long-term (Collins and Menon). ✓
That is the correct answer.
